title: ranunculus occidentalis subsp. turneri
intro: Ranunculus occidentalis subsp. turneri, also known as Turner's buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Ranunculus genus and is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. Traditionally, Turner's buttercup has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and reducing pain. The health benefits of Turner's buttercup include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, allowing for a range of applications, from topical treatments to internal remedies.
